observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
Transfer the water supply-related assets to the books of the local Economic Enterprise to have a more accurate picture of the revenue generation operation.
Henceforth, always correlates the incurred expenditures to its corresponding revenue to achieve fairness in the presentation of Financial Statements and avoid the possibility of misleading information.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare Adjusting Journal Entries to record the transfer of all the unexpended DRRM Fund in the Special Trust Fund from the General Fund Book;
Maintain subsidiary ledgers for the transfers of unutilized LDRRMF and other sources of such funds to the special trust fund by year of transfer; and
Henceforth, strict compliance with the accounting and reporting guidelines on the disbursement of unexpended LDRRMF is enjoined.

We recommended that Management require the following:
Local Health Board to submit the list of frontline HRHs and public HWs, including BHWs, in relation to AHDP and SRA grant.
HRMO to submit the authority of HRHs and public HWs to physically report in the workstation on the period duration of the subject benefits, including the unsubmitted individual DTRs.
MHO to submit copies of medical journals to support the claims of SRA. Otherwise, the total amount of benefits will be disallowed in the audit.

We recommended that Management submit a written explanation justifying why the above-mentioned guidelines in granting Active Hazard Duty Pay and Special Risk Allowance have not been adhered. Henceforth, always ensure that applicable policies appropriate in dealing with the affairs of the LGU shall be observed to avoid possible suspension or disallowance in the audit.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to return the excess or unutilized fund to its source agencies and instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary journal entries once an official receipt for the refund by the source agencies is received to ensure the fair presentation of the accounts in the Financial Statement.
We recommended that Management immediately remit the remaining balances of the Priority Development Assistance Fund (PDAF) to the Bureau of the Treasury.

We recommended that Management:
Properly address the issues and concerns that hindered the implementation of these projects so that PPAs can all be implemented and carried out as planned; and
Review the balance of the continuing appropriation for this fund to determine savings from completed projects. Further, evaluate and re-assess the PPAs if they are still responsive to the priority needs of the constituents of the Municipality, otherwise, reprogram the fund consistent with Paragraph 2, Section 322 of the Local Government Code.

We recommended that Management direct the concerned HRMO personnel to properly review the applications for monetization prior to processing the same. Henceforth, ensure strict compliance with the laws, rules, and regulations on the monetization of leave credits, specifically Sections 22 and 23 of the Omnibus Rules on Leave, Rule XVI Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of EO No. 29

We recommended that Management direct the:

Municipal Treasurer to prepare a schedule of unreleased/ unclaimed checks at the end of the reporting period, and submit thereafter to the Municipal Accountant.

Municipal Accountant to post adjustments in the books of accounts that corresponds to the unreleased or unclaimed checks at year-end.

Recommendations:

Require the Municipal Accountant to:

Adjust the misstatement of the Accumulated Depreciation accordingly to rectify the errors of calculation and to present the Road Network account to its correct carrying amount;

Consistently review the correctness of the computation of depreciation and appropriately maintain Road network accounts in adherence to the mentioned guidelines.

Henceforth, updates themselves of the regulations in the proper keeping of accounts, specifically on the depreciation of road network assets.

Recommendations:

Require the Municipal Engineer to provide a complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.

For existing road networks, direct the Municipal Engineer to coordinate with the Municipal Assessor in the determination of the fair value of road lot component for all local roads under the jurisdiction of the Municipal Government.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to recognize the amount pertaining to the road lot as part of the reported book value of the Road Networks account.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with appropriate personnel, to recognize in the books of accounts the receipt of donated communication equipment. Henceforth, always ensure that proper coordination among the Offices concerning all future receipt of donations.

We recommended and the Management agreed to strengthen its monitoring controls by requiring the personnel in-charge of donations to:

Ensure issuance of Acknowledgment Receipts to all future receipts of donations in-kind to confirm that donations are properly recorded and accounted for; 

Ensure that all future issuances of donated items are properly supported with Issuance Form for In-Kind Donations or any document/record to prove receipt by and distribution to the intended beneficiaries; and

Determine the inventory of remaining undistributed items for recording in the Registry of Donated Relief Goods for DRRM.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer to 

Update themselves to the latest policies and guidelines on Donations In-Kind as provided by the relevant and competent government offices; and

Ensure the on-time preparation and submission of the Report of the Donations Received, Distributed and Balances to the Office of the Civil Defense and Auditor’s Office, in compliance to Section 2(b) of COA Circular 2020-009, moving forward.

We recommended Management to strengthen controls over the procurement of drugs and medicines by ensuring that all the necessary documentations are being asked for and complied with to assure that the acquisitions thereof concur to be of the best quality, clinically-proven effective and safe for consumption.

We recommended Management to purchase drugs and medicines that are within the Philippine National Drug Formulary Volume I.  If the needed medicines were not in the list, write a letter to the Head of the National Drug Policy Office appealing request to purchase. Henceforth, strict compliance of Administrative Order (AO) No. 2015-0051, Executive Order (EO) No. 49, and Philippine National Drug Formulary (PNDF) Volume I.

We recommended that Management adhere to the procurement process in the implementation of programs and activities to address the COVID-19 provided in RA 9184 and GPPB Circular 01-2020, and discourage the grant cash advances to disbursing officers for the procurement of goods in compliance with COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended and the Management agreed to religiously comply with all the reportorial requirements provided for the utilization of public funds, such as BGCM, to attain a higher degree of transparency of information and comply with the full disclosure policy required by the oversight committees.

We recommended that Management instruct the personnel concerned to submit copies of contracts of the above-stated procurements immediately. Moreover, strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Engineer to require the contractors requesting advance payment/mobilization fee to post/submit a standby letter of credit or a guarantee/security instrument prior to processing the payment thereof. Henceforth, always ensure to observe strict compliance for all matters relevant in the implementation of infrastructure projects.

We recommended the Management to:

Require the contractors of 7 infrastructure project to post warranty security to guarantee their performance in the event of eventual structural defects/failures; and

Henceforth, strictly observe and enforce the provisions required by RA 9184 and its IRR specifically its requirements on the posting of warranty securities.

We recommended Management to ensure proper recording of financial transactions by requiring the: 

Municipal Treasurer to-

Furnish the Municipal Accountant’s Office, copies of the previous and current notarized contract of lease of stallholders; 

Municipal Accountant to-

Prepare appropriate adjusting entries to record the uncollected balances from stallholders as of December 31, 2019; and 

Ensure that the succeeding revenues from the contract of lease will be recorded in accordance with accrual basis and Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards.

We recommended Management to instruct the Municipal Treasurer, with coordination of the Sangguniang Bayan, to ensure that local tax rates are updated every five years pursuant to Section 191 of the Local Government Code, with the end view of increasing the revenue collections of the Municipality.

We recommended and the Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the concerned Offices in ensuring that local fees and charges are updated regularly, with the end view of increasing the revenue collections of the Municipality.

We recommended Management to Instruct the Municipal Engineer to:

Coordinate with appropriate government agencies or individuals for the conduct of the Water Potability Tests to the three SALINTUBIG Projects. The result will determine if the water in these projects is suitable for drinking and cooking purposes. 

In the succeeding SALINTUBIG Projects, ensure that the Water Potability Tests are made before and during construction before they are operated and opened for public use, and regular periodic conduct of the same after completion as part of the project's sustainability.

We recommended that Management strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 and instruct the Municipal Accountant not to grant cash advances to non-permanent employees, to avoid unnecessary loss of government funds.

We recommended Management to instruct the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the provisions of the law regarding the mandated monthly net take-home pay for government employees to boost the morale and productivity of its employees in government service.

We recommended Management to:

Require the Municipal Accountant, Property Officer, Municipal Treasurer to fast-track the reconciliation of their records and thereafter, ensure that these balances are reconciled at year-end.

Ensure that the Property Officer, together with the Inventory Committee, conduct an actual physical count of PPEs, annually.

Instruct the Municipal Treasurer or designated personnel to prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and cause the immediate disposal of unserviceable assets following the pertinent guidelines in supply and property management.

We recommended that Management require the Local School Board to include the Early Childhood Care and Development programs in the formulation of SEF Budget in the ensuing year to promote optimum growth and development of our young children and families.

We recommended that Management:

Fast-track the procurement of the necessary materials and equipment of the unimplemented, including the partially implemented projects, otherwise, require the Focal Person to secure written approval from the DOLE-RO for possible changes or deviations from the approved project proposal;  

Instruct the Focal Person to undertake proper and regular monitoring of DOLE-Funded Projects and immediately address the problems that it may encounter; and
In the succeeding submission of project proposals, require the Focal Person to thoroughly review the project location, cost breakdown portion of the proposal and ensure that the identified materials and equipment are available and properly estimated.

We recommended Management to:

Require the appropriate office to ensure that all Lands owned by the municipality are duly registered under the name LGU Lingig and secure copies of the corresponding Torrens Certificate of Title (TCT). 

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to exert best efforts to trace the acquisition of the unrecorded real properties and determine the appropriate cost to be recognized in the financial statements.

We recommended Management to:

Require the MPDO to coordinate with the appropriate government agency and develop LGU Lingig’s website. 

Subsequently, ensure that the vital information of LGU, such as financial reports, infrastructure projects, and transparency seal, among others, be posted in its official website, in compliance with the provisions of GAA of 2018 and RA 9184.

We recommended that Management require the MDRRM Officer to:

Install mechanism that will monitor the implementation of the PAPs identified in the DRRM Plan and fast-track its implementation,

Conduct in-depth studies for previously identified project and determine if these are still feasible and practicable to be implemented, otherwise, realign these funds to other disaster related PPAs which are implementable within the year.

Optimally utilize funds for EIC materials and consider other strategies for mass awareness and distribution of information materials through print and non-print.

We recommended that Management ensure the optimal utilization of the 20% DF in accordance with DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 by:

Requiring in-depth study of the proposed programs and projects before inclusion in the Annual Investment Plan.

Prioritizing projects which are doable, realizable and within the capability of the LGU to implement within the targeted completion period.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed to ensure proper recording of financial transactions by requiring the:
Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator-
Furnish the Municipal Accountant’s Office, the updated list of LWSS Consumers with uncollected Waterworks System Fees as of December 31, 2019;
In subsequent months, furnish the Municipal Accountant’s Office the Monthly Billing Reports;
Municipal Accountant to-
Prepare appropriate adjusting entries to record the Accounts Receivable from Lingig Waterworks System Consumers as of December 31, 2019; and
Ensure that the succeeding revenues from the Waterworks System Fees will be recorded in accordance with accrual basis and Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards.

We recommended the Management to instruct the Municipal Accountant to
Prepare adjusting entries in the books of accounts to reclassify the high-yield savings account into its appropriate line item in the Financial Statements; and
Ensure that financial transactions are recorded in accordance with the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards to assure reliability on the fair presentation of the Financial Statements.

We recommended Management to ensure proper recording of financial transactions by requiring the:
Municipal Treasurer to-
Furnish the Municipal Accountant’s Office, copies of the previous and current notarized contract of lease of stallholders;
Municipal Accountant to-
Prepare appropriate adjusting entries to record the uncollected balances from stallholders as of December 31, 2019; and
Ensure that the succeeding revenues from the contract of lease will be recorded in accordance with accrual basis and Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards.

We recommended Management to:
Regulate the roaming-around fish and meat vendors within the Municipality and penalize them in consonance with pertinent ordinances;
Designate specific Office or Officer to manage/ maintain the cleanliness and sanitation of the Public Market and Terminal;
Require the Municipal Treasurer to strengthen the control measures in collecting the stall rental fees by providing a contract of Lease between the LGU and the Public Market Stallholders; and
Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to strictly impose the prompt collection of the balances from the delinquent stallholders in consonance with the 2013 Revised Revenue Code of Lingig.

We recommended Management to instruct the Municipal Treasurer, with coordination of the Sangguniang Bayan, to ensure that local tax rates are updated every five years pursuant to Section 191 of the Local Government Code, with the end view of increasing the revenue collections of the Municipality.

We recommended and the Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the concerned Offices in ensuring that local fees and charges are updated regularly, with the end view of increasing the revenue collections of the Municipality.

We recommended Management to Instruct the Municipal Engineer to:
Coordinate with appropriate government agencies or individuals for the conduct of the Water Potability Tests to the three SALINTUBIG Projects. The result will determine if the water in these projects is suitable for drinking and cooking purposes.
In the succeeding SALINTUBIG Projects, ensure that the Water Potability Tests are made before and during construction before they are operated and opened for public use, and regular periodic conduct of the same after completion as part of the project's sustainability.

We recommended that Management strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 and instruct the Municipal Accountant not to grant cash advances to non-permanent employees, to avoid unnecessary loss of government funds.

We recommended Management to instruct the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the provisions of the law regarding the mandated monthly net take-home pay for government employees to boost the morale and productivity of its employees in government service.

We recommended Management to:

Require the Municipal Accountant, Property Officer, Municipal Treasurer to fast-track the reconciliation of their records and thereafter, ensure that these balances are reconciled at year-end.

Ensure that the Property Officer, together with the Inventory Committee, conduct an actual physical count of PPEs, annually.

Instruct the Municipal Treasurer or designated personnel to prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and cause the immediate disposal of unserviceable assets following the pertinent guidelines in supply and property management.

In addition, the remittances should be cross-checked with the daily collections and remittance reports to ensure that all collections are reported and remitted during the period these were collected.

We recommended Management to:

Require the appropriate office to ensure that all Lands owned by the municipality are duly registered under the name LGU Lingig and secure copies of the corresponding Torrens Certificate of Title (TCT). 

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to exert best efforts to trace the acquisition of the unrecorded real properties and determine the appropriate cost to be recognized in the financial statements.

observations.reason: Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to Require Municipal Planning and Development Officer and all respective Departments concerned to fully comply with full disclosure policy as required under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2013-140 in order to inform the constituents of LGU Lingig and the general public of its accomplishments and how public funds were utilized.

We recommended that Management require the MDRRM Officer to:

Install mechanism that will monitor the implementation of the PAPs identified in the DRRM Plan and fast-track its implementation,

Conduct in-depth studies for previously identified project and determine if these are still feasible and practicable to be implemented, otherwise, realign these funds to other disaster related PPAs which are implementable within the year.

Optimally utilize funds for EIC materials and consider other strategies for mass awareness and distribution of information materials through print and non-print.
observations.reason: Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management ensure the optimal utilization of the 20% DF in accordance with DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 by:

Requiring in-depth study of the proposed programs and projects before inclusion in the Annual Investment Plan.

Prioritizing projects which are doable, realizable and within the capability of the LGU to implement within the targeted completion period.
observations.reason: Partially Implemented
Partially Implemented

observations.managementAction: Created an Inventory Committee; Renew Acknowledge-ment Receipt of Equipment (ARE)
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management and the Local Development Council re-evaluate the priority programs and projects based on the approved Local Development Plan of the Municipality to optimally utilized its 20% Development Fund for the attainment of socio-economic development and environmental management. In addition, fast track the completion of the non-completed and non-implemented priority projects. Furthermore, strictly observe the provisions under DILG DBM JMC No. 2017-1.

We recommended that Management strictly comply with the minimum requirements for the operation of controlled dumps; maintain access road for easy and safe transport of solid waste; include in its Ecological Solid Waste Management Program appropriation for the establishment of sanitary landfill; and to prioritize the implementation of solid waste management program by providing enough budget in order to realize the programs and projects under SWMP.

We recommended that Management conduct intensive information and education campaign on the proper segregation of solid wastes; enforce mandatory segregation of solid wastes at the source; and strictly comply with the minimum requirements for segregation and storage of solid waste pending collection as mandated by RA 9003 or Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000.

We recommended that Management instruct the manager of the Local Economic Enterprise to prepare a 5-Year Business Plan for each local economic enterprise maintained and operated by the local government unit as basis for the operationalization of the annual operating budget of each LEEs to ensure that programs, activities and projects will contribute to its effective and efficient operation and the achievement of the LEEs goals and objectives.

We recommended that Management instruct the manager of the Local Economic Enterprise to prepare a 5-Year Business Plan for each local economic enterprise maintained and operated by the local government unit as basis for the operationalization of the annual operating budget of each LEEs to ensure that programs, activities and projects will contribute to its effective and efficient operation and the achievement of the LEEs goals and objectives.

We recommended Management to:
Require the Municipal Accountant, Property Officer, Municipal Treasurer to fast-track the reconciliation of their records and thereafter, ensure that these balances are reconciled at year-end.
Ensure that the Property Officer, together with the Inventory Committee, conduct an actual physical count of PPEs, annually.
Instruct the Municipal Treasurer or designated personnel to prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and cause the immediate disposal of unserviceable assets following the pertinent guidelines in supply and property management.
During the exit conference, the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant acknowledged the observations and agreed to exert efforts to implement the recommendations within this year.

We recommended Management to strengthen the internal controls in handling cash collections and deposits of the LGU, and require the Municipal Treasurer/liquidating officer to deposit collections promptly and intact. In addition, the remittances should be cross-checked with the daily collections and remittance reports to ensure that all collections are reported and remitted during the period these were collected.
The Management, through the Municipal Treasurer, assured that they will comply with the audit recommendations.

We recommended that Management require the Local School Board to include the Early Childhood Care and Development programs in the formulation of SEF Budget in the ensuing year to promote optimum growth and development of our young children and families.
During the exit conference, the Management represented by the MPDO as member of the Local School Board, ensures that LGU include allocations for ECCD programs, although not coming from SEF Budget due to limited resources, however allocations were provided under GAD and LCPC Budget.

We recommended that Management:
Fast-track the procurement of the necessary materials and equipment of the unimplemented, including the partially implemented projects, otherwise, require the Focal Person to secure written approval from the DOLE-RO for possible changes or deviations from the approved project proposal;
Instruct the Focal Person to undertake proper and regular monitoring of DOLE-Funded Projects and immediately address the problems that it may encounter; and
In the succeeding submission of project proposals, require the Focal Person to thoroughly review the project location, cost breakdown portion of the proposal and ensure that the identified materials and equipment are available and properly estimated.
The Management, during the conduct of the exit conference, acknowledged the observations and agreed to comply with the above recommendations.

We recommended Management to:
Require the appropriate office to ensure that all Lands owned by the municipality are duly registered under the name LGU Lingig and secure copies of the corresponding Torrens Certificate of Title (TCT).
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to exert best efforts to trace the acquisition of the unrecorded real properties and determine the appropriate cost to be recognized in the financial statements.
The Management, through the Municipal Accountant, commented that the Office of the Accountant will secure records from the Municipal Assessors Office regarding the unrecorded real properties so that they can be recorded in the books.

We recommended Management to:
Require the MPDO to coordinate with the appropriate government agency and develop LGU Lingig’s website.
Subsequently, ensure that the vital information of LGU, such as financial reports, infrastructure projects, and transparency seal, among others, be posted in its official website, in compliance with the provisions of GAA of 2018 and RA 9184.
As discussed during the exit conference, the Management, thru its SB Secretary and MPDO are willing to be part in the development of LGU’s Official Website.

We recommended Management to Require Municipal Planning and Development Officer and all respective Departments concerned to fully comply with full disclosure policy as required under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2013-140 in order to inform the constituents of LGU Lingig and the general public of its accomplishments and how public funds were utilized.
The Management admitted that LGU Lingig has not fully complied with the Full Disclosure Policy and assured full compliance of the audit recommendations.

We recommended that Management require the MDRRM Officer to:
Install mechanism that will monitor the implementation of the PAPs identified in the DRRM Plan and fast-track its implementation,
Conduct in-depth studies for previously identified project and determine if these are still feasible and practicable to be implemented, otherwise, realign these funds to other disaster related PPAs which are implementable within the year.
Optimally utilize funds for EIC materials and consider other strategies for mass awareness and distribution of information materials through print and non-print.

We recommended that Management ensure the optimal utilization of the 20% DF in accordance with DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 by:
Requiring in-depth study of the proposed programs and projects before inclusion in the Annual Investment Plan.
Prioritizing projects which are doable, realizable and within the capability of the LGU to implement within the targeted completion period.
The Management, thru its MPDO responded that he, together with the Local Development Council, will implement strategies and policies that will ensure optimal utilization of the 20% DF and agreed to comply with the above audit recommendations.

observations.managementAction: Created an Inventory Committee; Renew Acknowledgement Receipt of Equipment (ARE)
observations.reason: Incomplete Physical Count of PPEs

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management and the Local Development Council re-evaluate the priority programs and projects based on the approved Local Development Plan of the Municipality to optimally utilized its 20% Development Fund for the attainment of socio-economic development and environmental management. In addition, fast track the completion of the non-completed and non-implemented priority projects. Furthermore, strictly observe the provisions under DILG DBM JMC No. 2017-1.
observations.reason: Reiterated in 2018 AAR

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly observe the implementation of the programs, projects and activities for the Local Council for the Protection of Children including its mandated appropriation from the LGUs Internal Revenue Allotment, and instruct the LCPC to prepare the Local Development Plan for Children as basis for the incorporation of PPAs to the Annual Investment Plan of the Municipality.
observations.reason: The LGU Lingig appropriated 1% of its IRA for LCPC in 2018 Annual Budget

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly comply with the minimum requirements for the operation of controlled dumps; maintain access road for easy and safe transport of solid waste; include in its Ecological Solid Waste Management Program appropriation for the establishment of sanitary landfill; and to prioritize the implementation of solid waste management program by providing enough budget in order to realize the programs and projects under SWMP.
observations.reason: Concreting of access road going to Eco-park/dump site is currently on-going which is funded from Assistance to Municipalities.  While establishment of sanitary landfill is not yet a priority at the moment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management conduct intensive information and education campaign on the proper segregation of solid wastes; enforce mandatory segregation of solid wastes at the source; and strictly comply with the minimum requirements for segregation and storage of solid waste pending collection as mandated by RA 9003 or Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000.
observations.reason: There are still some areas or barangays who does not comply.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management strictly observe the provisions of the law regarding the mandated monthly net take home pay for government employees to boast the morale and productivity of its employees in government service.
observations.reason: Although payroll sheets still showed net pay below P4,000, loan applications are already monitored upon renewal.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (LDRRMC) instruct the MDRRM Officer to prepare project implementation schedule and/or work and financial plan for each PPAs funded under the LDRRM Fund and ensure that programming of unexpended LDRRM Fund of previous years’ be properly included in the LDRRM Investment Plan of the Municipality.
observations.reason: LDRRMF Investment Plan was already prepared including unexpended fund of prior years.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management create the mandatory positions for LDRRMO and appoint personnel who met the qualification standards set in the NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014 in order to institutionalize the LDRRMO’s organizational structure and manpower compliment aimed to carry out the DRRM plans and programs in the long run.
observations.reason: Regular position for LDRRMO was already created and appropriated in CY2018

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the concerned offices to prepare work and financial plan and/or project implementation schedule for each PPAs funded from various National Government Agencies and observe the timeline for its implementation based on the policies of these programs.  Furthermore, we recommend to fast-track the implementation of the projects.
observations.reason: Projects funded from NGAs were implemented and fast-tracked especially ADM/AM, Salintubig, BUB-LGSF

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
Create the Inventory Committee of the LGU and exert extra effort to conduct the physical count of all PPE; prepare the RPCPPE; and reconcile the report with the accounting records;

Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the inventory of unserviceable properties, if any, based on the RPCPPE for appropriate action; and prepare and/or renew the Acknowledgement Receipt of Equipment (ARE) to the actual holder of the PPE items; and

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and update the PPE Ledger Cards; and to effect adjusting entries after reconciliation of PPE records with the RPCPPE.
observations.managementAction: The Management instructed the Municipal Treasurer to conduct the physical inventory of all PPEs and reconcile with the Municipal Accounting Office.
observations.reason: The Municipality still failed to conduct the periodic physical count of the PPEs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the GAD Focal Point System to spearhead the establishment of a GAD Database that could aid in gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation. Moreover, direct the GAD Focal Point persons to attend GAD trainings and seminars to increase their knowledge and understanding in the formulation of GAD database.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2017, GAD database was not yet established

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management and the Local Development Council re-evaluate the priority programs and projects based on the approved Local Development Plan of the Municipality to optimally utilized its 20% Development Fund for the attainment of socio-economic development and environmental management. In addition, fast track the completion of the non-completed and non-implemented priority projects. Furthermore, strictly observe the provisions under DILG DBM JMC No. 2017-1.

We recommended that Management strictly observe the implementation of the programs, projects and activities for the Local Council for the Protection of Children including its mandated appropriation from the LGUs Internal Revenue Allotment, and instruct the LCPC to prepare the Local Development Plan for Children as basis for the incorporation of PPAs to the Annual Investment Plan of the Municipality.

We recommended that Management strictly comply with the minimum requirements for the operation of controlled dumps; maintain access road for easy and safe transport of solid waste; include in its Ecological Solid Waste Management Program appropriation for the establishment of sanitary landfill; and to prioritize the implementation of solid waste management program by providing enough budget in order to realize the programs and projects under SWMP.

We recommended that Management conduct intensive information and education campaign on the proper segregation of solid wastes; enforce mandatory segregation of solid wastes at the source; and strictly comply with the minimum requirements for segregation and storage of solid waste pending collection as mandated by RA 9003 or Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000.

We recommended that the Management strictly observe the provisions of the law regarding the mandated monthly net take home pay for government employees to boast the morale and productivity of its employees in government service.

We recommended that Management instruct the manager of the Local Economic Enterprise to prepare a 5-Year Business Plan for each local economic enterprise maintained and operated by the local government unit as basis for the operationalization of the annual operating budget of each LEEs to ensure that programs, activities and projects will contribute to its effective and efficient operation and the achievement of the LEEs goals and objectives.

We recommended that Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (LDRRMC) instruct the MDRRM Officer to prepare project implementation schedule and/or work and financial plan for each PPAs funded under the LDRRM Fund and ensure that programming of unexpended LDRRM Fund of previous years’ be properly included in the LDRRM Investment Plan of the Municipality.

We recommended that Management create the mandatory positions for LDRRMO and appoint personnel who met the qualification standards set in the NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014 in order to institutionalize the LDRRMO’s organizational structure and manpower compliment aimed to carry out the DRRM plans and programs in the long run.

We recommended that Management instruct the concerned offices to prepare work and financial plan and/or project implementation schedule for each PPAs funded from various National Government Agencies and observe the timeline for its implementation based on the policies of these programs.  Furthermore, we recommend to fast-track the implementation of the projects.

observations.managementAction: The Management instructed the Municipal Accountant to prepare the subsidiary ledgers for all Construction in Progress accounts.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant failed to submit the subsidiary ledger for verification.  The CIP accounts were updated and yet tobe coordinated to the Municipal Engineering Office to determine the completed projects.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to strictly observe the revised implementing rules and regulations of the Procurement Law;

The BAC Secretariat promptly prepare the minutes of meeting of the Committee in accordance to the RIRR of RA No. 9184; and

The Technical Working Group (TWG) to meticulously verify and validate the eligibility documents of the bidder who was declared as the Lowest Calculated Bidder, and to ensure that additional requirements were submitted before declaring the bidder as the Lowest Calculated and Responsive Bidder.
observations.managementAction: The BAC Chairman assured to follow the rules and regulations of RA 9184
observations.reason: The BAC strictly followed the guidelines provided in RA 9184

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

The end-user offices to meticulously and judiciously prepare the Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) in accordance with Section 7.3.2 of the RIRR of the RA No. 9184 to avoid delay in the implementation of programs and projects;

The BAC Secretariat and the BAC Chairperson to meticulously check the PPMPs prepared by the end-user units before consolidating it to the APP to facilitate the efficient conduct of procurement activities for every procurement project; and

The Municipal Accountant ensure that PPMPs prepared by the end-user units were in accordance to the RIRR of the RA No. 9184 before certifying the correctness of the said Plan.
observations.managementAction: The end-user offices were instructed to prepare the PPMP, and the Budget Officer the APP.
observations.reason: Not all offices prepared their PPMPs.  In addition, PPMPs did not reconcile with APP.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management meticulously and judiciously plan the projects to assure the achievement of its goals, objectives and the expected outputs, and to ensure the sustainability of the project for the upliftment of the livelihood and income of its constituents. In addition, instruct the focal point person of every projects fund by the funds from National Government Agencies to submit the accomplishment and progress reports to validate the implementation of projects.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2017, there are some livelihood projects that were not implemented

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the local ESWM Board to:

Fast-track the application of Environmental Compliance Certificate (ECC) to make use of its newly designated dump site and to implement the pending programs and projects for solid waste management;

Conduct intensive information and education campaign on the proper segregation of solid wastes; and

Submit the 10-year Ecological Solid Waste Management Plan of the LGU.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2017, there are still deficiencies which need to be improved.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management stop the practice of charging to the 20% development fund expenses which are not related to and do not partake the nature of investment/capital expenditures. The municipal officials are enjoined to follow strictly the guidelines set under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 of the DBM and DILG in the utilization of the 20% Development Fund to ensure its optimal utilization.
observations.reason: The 20% development fund was focused to finance the implementation of capital expenditures.

Review of the CY2017 appropriations there are still PPAs stated in generic terms.  The Management disclosed that it is inevitable to appropriate programs in generic terms due to the difficulty of specifying the PPAs in advance which may result to re-alignment and non-implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local School Board prepare the Special Education Fund (SEF) Plan and Budget prioritizing the programs, projects and activities that could enhance the development of education and improvement of its schools.  Moreover, stop charging administrative expenses of the District Office to the Special Education Fund (SEF).
observations.reason: The LSB did not in any way charged expenditures which are ineligible to the fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the LGUs Report of Utilization on LDRRMF and transfer the remaining unexpended LDRRMF balance, if any, to the Special Trust Fund.  In addition, reconcile the unutilized LDRRMF balance per Report on Utilization of LDRRMF to the recorded amount in the Trust Liability – DRRM in the Financial Statements.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant transferred the LDRRM Fund balance to Special Trust Fund.
As of the conduct of cash examination, there are still revenue collectors who incurred delay in the remittance of their collections.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Municipal Accountant should strictly adhere to the prescribed time frame for the submission of monthly and quarterly reports.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2017, all DVs and its supporting documents were forwarded.  However, still incurred delay on the submission.
Some Purchase Orders and their supporting documents were not completely filled-up with the necessary information.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to strictly adhere to the guidelines on the identification of eligible projects for funding under the confidential fund to avoid the granting of cash advances in excess of the authorized amount.
observations.managementAction: Management did not submit course of action to implement the recommendation.
observations.reason: The LCE was unable to adhere the rules and guidelines on the appropriate amount to be computed as confidential fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management instruct the agency accountant or the authorized representative to submit the cash advance disbursement voucher and the necessary documents within the prescribed period for post audit of the cash advance for confidential fund.
observations.managementAction: Management did not submit course of action to implement the recommendation.
observations.reason: Submission of the documents and its supporting documents on the cash advance drawn for confidential expenses remained way beyond the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit promptly all paid DVs and payrolls together with the supporting documents pursuant to Section 39 (1) (3) of PD 1445 and Section 7.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006.

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to forward the DVs to the Accounting Office as soon as the corresponding checks are released to the suppliers/payee, to ensure timely submission thereof to the Auditor.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant was instructed to submit the DVs and its supporting documents promptly.
observations.reason: Financial remained way beyond the legal due date.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management direct the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to render overtime services and prioritize the reconciliation of the PPE accounts in order to submit the RPCPPE to the Office of the Auditor
observations.managementAction: The MTO instructed to conduct the Physical count of PPEs.
observations.reason:
No physical count was conducted for CY 2016. See observation #1 for full discussion.

Despite of the annual recommendation to conduct physical inventory taking, management did not give importance on the impact of this undertaking to the financial position of the LGU.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
Create the Inventory Committee of the LGU and exert extra effort to conduct the physical count of all PPE; prepare the RPCPPE; and reconcile the report with the accounting records;
Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the inventory of unserviceable properties, if any, based on the RPCPPE for appropriate action; and prepare and/or renew the Acknowledgement Receipt of Equipment (ARE) to the actual holder of the PPE items; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and update the PPE Ledger Cards; and to effect adjusting entries after reconciliation of PPE records with the RPCPPE.

3.4		We recommended that:
The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to strictly observe the revised implementing rules and regulations of the Procurement Law;
The BAC Secretariat promptly prepare the minutes of meeting of the Committee in accordance to the RIRR of RA No. 9184; and
The Technical Working Group (TWG) to meticulously verify and validate the eligibility documents of the bidder who was declared as the Lowest Calculated Bidder, and to ensure that additional requirements were submitted before declaring the bidder as the Lowest Calculated and Responsive Bidder.

4.6  	We recommended that:
The end-user offices to meticulously and judiciously prepare the Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) in accordance with Section 7.3.2 of the RIRR of the RA No. 9184 to avoid delay in the implementation of programs and projects;
The BAC Secretariat and the BAC Chairperson to meticulously check the PPMPs prepared by the end-user units before consolidating it to the APP to facilitate the efficient conduct of procurement activities for every procurement project; and
The Municipal Accountant ensure that PPMPs prepared by the end-user units were in accordance to the RIRR of the RA No. 9184 before certifying the correctness of the said Plan.

5.6	We recommended that Management meticulously and judiciously plan the projects to assure the achievement of its goals, objectives and the expected outputs, and to ensure the sustainability of the project for the upliftment of the livelihood and income of its constituents. In addition, instruct the focal point person of every projects fund by the funds from National Government Agencies to submit the accomplishment and progress reports to validate the implementation of projects.

6.5	We recommended that Management require the GAD Focal Point System to spearhead the establishment of a GAD Database that could aid in gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation. Moreover, direct the GAD Focal Point persons to attend GAD trainings and seminars to increase their knowledge and understanding in the formulation of GAD database.

We recommended that the local ESWM Board to:
Fast-track the application of Environmental Compliance Certificate (ECC) to make use of its newly designated dump site and to implement the pending programs and projects for solid waste management;
Conduct intensive information and education campaign on the proper segregation of solid wastes; and
Submit the 10-year Ecological Solid Waste Management Plan of the LGU.

8.6	We recommended that management stop the practice of charging to the 20% development fund expenses which are not related to and do not partake the nature of investment/capital expenditures. The municipal officials are enjoined to follow strictly the guidelines set under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 of the DBM and DILG in the utilization of the 20% Development Fund to ensure its optimal utilization.

10.5	We recommended that the Local School Board prepare the Special Education Fund (SEF) Plan and Budget prioritizing the programs, projects and activities that could enhance the development of education and improvement of its schools.  Moreover, stop charging administrative expenses of the District Office to the Special Education Fund (SEF).

11.4  	We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the LGUs Report of Utilization on LDRRMF and transfer the remaining unexpended LDRRMF balance, if any, to the Special Trust Fund.  In addition, reconcile the unutilized LDRRMF balance per Report on Utilization of LDRRMF to the recorded amount in the Trust Liability – DRRM in the Financial Statements.
13.4	We recommended that Municipal Accountant should strictly adhere to the prescribed time frame for the submission of monthly and quarterly reports.

We recommended management to carefully scrutinize and evaluate the eligibility of the programs, projects and activities proposed for inclusion in the Peace and Order Program to avoid excessive appropriation for Confidential Fund.
observations.managementAction: The LCE to re-inspect the items which are not eligible to be included in the POP.
observations.reason: There are still non-allowable expenditures formed part of the POP.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management stop the practice of charging to the 20% development fund expenses which are not related to and do not partake the nature of investment/capital expenditures. The municipal officials are enjoined to follow strictly the guidelines set under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 of the DBM and DILG in the utilization of the 20% Development Fund to ensure its optimal utilization.
observations.managementAction: The Management instructed the Accountant to stop charging expenses which are deemed ineligible to be charged to the 20% Development Fund.
observations.reason: This observation was reiterated since there were still ineligible expenditures charged to 20% Development Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant to record all fund transfers in the books as receivables to enable the LGU to evaluate whether the funds being transferred is utilized for its intended purpose.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant was advised to reclassify the said fund transfer to its appropriate accounts.
observations.reason: As of Dec. 31, 2016, the fund transfers were recorded to Due from LGU and Other Receivable Account.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

Submit program design and justification on the importance of the benchmarking.

Observe strictly the guidelines set by the DILG on Lakbay-Aral; and

Observe prudence in the disbursement of government funds and instead, appropriate the funds for project/activities/programs that bring immediate socio-economic benefits to local constituents.
observations.managementAction: The management stop the incurrence of unnecessary expenses during conferences and lakbay-aral was stopped.
observations.reason: For CY 2016, the LGU did not conduct lakbay-aral outside their jurisdiction.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterate our recommendation that management adhere strictly to the provisions of COA Circular 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 by:

Stopping the grant of additional cash advances to officers and employees who have still outstanding balance until such time that the previous cash advance given to him is first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made. 

Requiring the immediate liquidation of all long overdue unliquidated cash advances and refund the unexpended balance to the cashier/collecting officer who will then issue the necessary official receipt.

Requiring the Accountant to monitor regularly the cash advances and enforce the liquidation/ refund.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant deducted the payslip and other claims for benefits the balances of unliquidated cash advances.
observations.reason:
As of December 31, 2016 previous cash advances were not yet fully liquidated.

Although there was an effort made by management to enforce the liquidation of all cash advances, still there were minimal amounts remained unliquidated as of December 31, 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management instruct the Accountant to prepare a disbursement voucher remitting the unused PDAF to the Bureau of Treasury.
observations.managementAction: The MTO instructed to deposit the unused PDAF balance to the National Treasury Account.
observations.reason: The unutilized balances were fully returned to the National Treasury Account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to strictly observe the guidelines in the utilization of the Special Education Fund (SEF)provided under DECS-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 01 and 01-A.
observations.managementAction: The LSB instructed to stop the practice.
observations.reason: For CY 2016, the Municipal Accountant did not process ineligible expenditures charged to the SEF.
